2605.12426.title: Geometric Factual Recall in Transformers
2605.12426.paper_summary: How do transformer language models memorize factual associations? A common view casts internal weight matrices as associative memories over pairs of embeddings, requiring parameter counts that scale linearly with the number of facts. We develop a theoretical and empirical account of an alternative, \emph{geometric} form of memorization in which learned embeddings encode relational structure directly, and the MLP plays a qualitatively different role. In a controlled setting where a single-layer transformer must memorize random bijections from subjects to a shared attribute set, we prove that a logarithmic embedding dimension suffices: subject embeddings encode \emph{linear superpositions} of their associated attribute vectors, and a small MLP acts as a relation-conditioned selector that extracts the relevant attribute via ReLU gating, and not as an associative key-value mapping. We extend these results to the multi-hop setting -- chains of relational queries such as ``Who is the mother of the wife of $x$?'' -- providing constructions with and without chain-of-thought that exhibit a provable capacity-depth tradeoff, complemented by a matching information-theoretic lower bound. Empirically, gradient descent discovers solutions with precisely the predicted structure. Once trained, the MLP transfers zero-shot to entirely new bijections when subject embeddings are appropriately re-initialized, revealing that it has learned a generic selection mechanism rather than memorized any particular set of facts.
2605.12426.paper_summary_zh: 如何讓Transformer語言模型記住事實聯繫？一種常見的觀點將內部權重矩陣視為對嵌入對的聯想記憶，這需要與事實數量線性相關的參數計數。我們發展了一種理論和實證的替代方案，\emph{幾何}形式的記憶，其中學到的嵌入直接編碼關係結構，而多層感知機（MLP）則扮演著質量上不同的角色。在一個受控的環境中，單層Transformer必須記住從主體到共享屬性集的隨機雙射，我們證明了對數嵌入維度是足夠的：主體嵌入編碼其相關屬性向量的\emph{線性疊加}，而一個小的MLP則充當一個關係條件選擇器，通過ReLU閘提取相關屬性，而不是作為聯想鍵值映射。我們將這些結果擴展到多跳設置——例如“$x$的妻子的母親是誰？”這樣的關係查詢鏈——提供有和沒有思維鏈的構造，顯示出可證明的容量-深度權衡，並輔以匹配的信息理論下界。實證上，梯度下降發現的解決方案恰好具有預測的結構。一旦訓練完成，當主體嵌入適當重新初始化時，MLP可以零樣本轉移到全新的雙射，顯示它學會了一種通用的選擇機制，而不是記住任何特定的事實集合。
2605.12426.author: Shauli Ravfogel et.al.
2605.12426.authors: Shauli Ravfogel, Gilad Yehudai, Joan Bruna, Alberto Bietti
